scenes.voiceover: You think about today, not tomorrow.
You don't plan months or years ahead.
This short-term focus is common in the US, where instant gratification is built into everything.
But not planning ahead means you're always reacting to problems.
When something goes wrong, you have no cushion.
You scramble. Put it on credit.
This keeps you living paycheck to paycheck, one emergency from disaster.
Short-term thinking also means missing long-term opportunities.
You don't invest because you need money now.
But future you is going to need money too.
Your brain is wired to prioritize immediate needs.
Breaking this pattern means forcing long-term thinking.
Automate savings. Set up retirement contributions.
Build an emergency fund, even $10 at a time.
